title_slide: Poetry Dips Its Fingers In Every Colour

slides.heading: Poetry Dips Its Fingers In Every Colour
bullet_points: A data scientist's confession
The curiosity that drives both numbers and metaphor
Where poetry actually finds you
slides.speaker_note: Open with the contradiction — data scientist who cries at poems — to establish the personal stakes immediately.

slides.heading: Poetry Doesn't Stay in Its Lane
bullet_points: Not just literature classes and soft-cover journals
Human experience refuses to be sorted
The lyric form is the one that admits it
slides.speaker_note: Reframe poetry as something that intrudes — not something you have to seek out.

slides.heading: It Finds You in the Technical
bullet_points: Programmers call elegant code beautiful for a reason
The best commit messages carry something real
The boundary between technical and lyrical was always porous
slides.speaker_note: The server room example — poetry entered the workplace long before anyone invited it.

slides.heading: It Finds You in the Ordinary
bullet_points: Monday, 7:14 AM — burnt toast scraped over the sink
Poetry is ordinary experience seen clearly for one second
The grey commute counts. The blue Tuesday counts.
slides.speaker_note: Ground the audience in one hyper-specific sensory moment before expanding to the universal claim.

slides.heading: It Finds You in Grief
bullet_points: You don't miss 'a person' — you miss how they said your name
Poetry holds specificity without resolving it
That is not a small mercy
slides.speaker_note: This is the emotional core — deliver it slowly and let the silence sit before moving on.

slides.heading: It Finds You When You Stop Looking
bullet_points: Poetry comes sideways, not in elevated moments
A lyric heard forty times that suddenly means something different
The door doesn't need to be wide — a crack is enough
slides.speaker_note: End on permission — you don't have to be a 'poetry person' to let this in.

slides.heading: There's No Lesson. That's the Point.
bullet_points: Poetry doesn't sharpen your frameworks
It makes you someone who has stood inside a colour
The poem finds you — your only job is to not keep the door entirely shut
slides.speaker_note: Close with the anti-takeaway — the refusal to resolve is itself the message.
